title: Статика в generic-типе: своя на каждый закрытый тип

cards.prompt: A static field of a generic type is per closed constructed type: Counter<int>.Count and Counter<string>.Count are independent, so two int-Bumps print 1,2 and one string-Bump prints 1, and the final counters are 2 and 1
cards.code: using System; Console.WriteLine($"{Counter<int>.Bump()} {Counter<int>.Bump()} {Counter<string>.Bump()}"); Console.WriteLine($"{Counter<int>.Count} {Counter<string>.Count}"); class Counter<T> { public static int Count; public static int Bump() => ++Count; }

cards.prompt: A static constructor runs once per closed constructed type: Marker<int>.Id is 1 and Marker<string>.Id is 2 (the global counter advanced twice), and re-touching Marker<int> does not rerun its ctor so Inits stays 1, printing 1 2 1
cards.code: using System; var _ = Marker<int>.Id; var __ = Marker<string>.Id; var ___ = Marker<int>.Id; Console.WriteLine($"{Marker<int>.Id} {Marker<string>.Id} {Marker<int>.Inits}"); class Marker<T> { public static readonly int Id; public static int Inits; static Marker(){ Id = ++GlobalCtorCount.N; Inits++; } } static class GlobalCtorCount { public static int N; }

cards.prompt: Instances of the same closed constructed type share the static field: two Box<int> instances a and b share Box<int>.Total (2), and the Box<string> instance c incremented twice fills its own Box<string>.Total (2), printing 2 2
cards.code: using System; var a = new Box<int>(); var b = new Box<int>(); var c = new Box<string>(); a.Inc(); b.Inc(); c.Inc(); c.Inc(); Console.WriteLine($"{Box<int>.Total} {Box<string>.Total}"); class Box<T> { public static int Total; public void Inc() => Total++; }
